prevalence.source: WHO Europe / European dementia epidemiology syntheses
prevalence.trend: increasing due to aging demographics; some countries show slower growth due to risk factor control

burden.mortalityRate: High in older age groups (direct and indirect)
burden.source: IHME Global Burden of Disease (GBD) / WHO Europe

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis with cognitive testing (MMSE/MoCA) and functional assessment; imaging and biomarker pathways increasingly used in specialist settings

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: variable; many countries support opportunistic screening and memory clinic referral pathways
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Management generally strong with multidisciplinary support, though access varies by member state

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Memory clinics and specialist pathways common; increasing access to advanced diagnostics in some countries
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Non-pharmacologic care plans and caregiver support; cholinesterase inhibitors/memantine where indicated; management of vascular risks; social services integration
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Rising demand for long-term care and disparities in access between member states and rural vs urban areas
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Established multidisciplinary care models and strong social support services in many member states
